title: The Rival Hearth
intro: This hearth still burns. A hold no larger than Runehold keeps its Ember behind a stone door and a wary Circle of its own, and its Archon meets you on the threshold with a working half-shaped in her hand. "We know what you carry," she says. "We know what you want. Two Embers are stronger than one — but not if one hold swallows the other."

advisors.text: Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole. Treat with them, Archon. This is how the Flame comes home — not by conquest.
Or take theirs. One great fire, in one strong hold, is worth more than two frightened ones. They would do the same to us, given the nerve.

choices.label: Swear an accord between the two hearths

win.text: You clasp hands over both hearth-runes, and for a moment the two Embers lean toward each other like they remember being one Flame. An accord is sworn: not a gathering yet, but the first thread of one.

lose.text: The words come out too grasping, and the other Archon's door closes in your face. You have not made an enemy — but you have not made a friend, and the road home is long.

choices.label: Take their Ember by force of magic

win.text: It is quick, and it is ugly. You leave with two Embers where you came with one — and with the certain knowledge that every other hearth will now bar its door to Runehold, and sharpen something for you.

lose.text: Their Circle was ready, and yours was not. You are driven off with nothing, and word will run ahead of you: Runehold reaches for other holds' fires. That name will cost you.
